Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 13:32 ET
SPX negative gamma amplifies pre-NFP volatility as dispersion flows accelerate
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. SPX negative gamma at -$1.6B creates 2.5x amplification ahead of tomorrow's critical NFP print.
  • 2. Index puts (1.37 P/C) versus single-name calls (0.88 P/C) signals institutional dispersion hedging.
  • 3. Sector rotation accelerates into XLE calls while mega-cap tech faces death cross breakdowns.
NARRATIVE — pre-NFP negative gamma amplification

The most striking development is the structural dispersion setup unfolding ahead of NFP, with index P/C at 1.37 versus single-name P/C at 0.88 — a 0.49 divergence that screams institutional hedging. This pattern typically emerges when funds want downside protection on the index while maintaining individual stock upside exposure, particularly relevant given the 0.18 implied correlation reading that makes dispersion trades structurally attractive. SPX negative gamma at -$1.6B creates dangerous amplification dynamics into tomorrow's jobs data, with dealers positioned to accelerate any directional move beyond the $660.45 flip level. The massive SPY block activity — 350K puts at 655 strike and 331K calls at 658 — brackets current levels and suggests sophisticated positioning around potential NFP volatility, with institutions likely delta-hedging structured products rather than making directional bets. Energy rotation dominates the single-name landscape, with XLE posting an 84.4 technical score on golden cross momentum while massive sector call blocks flow (22K XLF Aug 53C, 15K XLE May 64C). This contrasts sharply with mega-cap tech facing death cross breakdowns (MSFT 19.6 score, META 21.2), creating the clearest sector dispersion opportunity since the Q4 rotation. The timing ahead of NFP suggests funds are positioning for either a growth scare (benefiting energy/financials) or continued expansion (benefiting the calls they're accumulating).

Layer 1: VIX 23.6Layer 2: SPX GEX -$1.6BLayer 3: Index P/C 1.37 vs single 0.88Layer 9: NFP tomorrow

GREEK PRESSURE KEY
+$M = dealer buys to re-hedge
−$M = dealer sells to re-hedge
Charm: time-decay drift only. Vanna: vol-shift only. Not blended.
